title: Surface canvas
goal: Paint onto an EditableImage attached to a Part's SurfaceGui.

steps.body:
`EditableImage` works just as well behind a `SurfaceGui` as behind
a `ScreenGui`. We'll put an EditableImage on the front face of a
Part and paint onto it with the mouse — like a wall-mounted
whiteboard in 3D space.
A flat Part to paint on.

The Paint LocalScript creates an EditableImage, checks that
`Mouse.Target` is our Part before painting, and maps the GUI-space
mouse position to pixel coordinates. The brush color rainbows over
time — click & drag to paint.
steps.explain:
`Mouse.Target` is the part under the cursor in 3D space — cheap
way to gate painting to only when the cursor is over the canvas.
For more accurate mapping on arbitrary faces, use `SurfaceGui`'s
local-space mouse position via a `BillboardGui`-style raycast.
steps.source:
local AssetService = game:GetService("AssetService")
local UserInputService = game:GetService("UserInputService")
local Players = game:GetService("Players")

local W, H = 512, 512
local BRUSH_R = 12

local label = script.Parent
local img = AssetService:CreateEditableImage({ Size = Vector2.new(W, H) })
img:DrawRectangle(Vector2.zero, img.Size, Color3.new(1, 1, 1), 0, Enum.ImageCombineType.Overwrite)
label.ImageContent = Content.fromObject(img)

local surfaceGui = label.Parent
local part = surfaceGui.Adornee or surfaceGui.Parent

local player = Players.LocalPlayer
local mouse = player:GetMouse()

local painting = false

local function tryPaint()
  if mouse.Target ~= part then return end
  local ap, as = label.AbsolutePosition, label.AbsoluteSize
  local fx = (mouse.X - ap.X) / as.X
  local fy = (mouse.Y - ap.Y) / as.Y
  if fx < 0 or fx > 1 or fy < 0 or fy > 1 then return end
  img:DrawCircle(
    Vector2.new(fx * W, fy * H), BRUSH_R,
    Color3.fromHSV(tick() % 1, 0.8, 1),
    0, Enum.ImageCombineType.AlphaBlend
  )
end

UserInputService.InputBegan:Connect(function(input, gp)
  if gp then return end
  if input.UserInputType == Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 then
    painting = true
    tryPaint()
  end
end)

UserInputService.InputEnded:Connect(function(input)
  if input.UserInputType == Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 then
    painting = false
  end
end)

mouse.Move:Connect(function()
  if painting then tryPaint() end
end)

steps.body:
Press ▶ **Play** (F5). Walk up to the white canvas and click & drag
to paint rainbow strokes directly onto the Part. Shift+F5 to stop.
